name: Los Pilones to Jerte - circular through the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve
name_original: Los Pilones a Jerte – circular por Reserva Natural Garganta de los Infiernos
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Jerte are set within the Jerte Valley, a region characterized by its mountainous terrain, deep gorges, and the Jerte River. The valley is flanked by the Sierra de Gredos and Sierra de Béjar y Candelario, offering significant elevation changes and diverse landscapes. Riders will encounter holm oaks, oak groves, and numerous waterfalls, providing varied backdrops for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails in Jerte The most popular mountain bike trail is Los Pilones to Jerte - circular through the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve, a 20.7 miles (33.3 km) difficult route that takes 3 hours 58 minutes to complete. This route explores the renowned Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is The Jerte Waterfalls - circular from Cabezuela del Valle, a difficult 21.8 miles (35.1 km) path. This trail features multiple waterfalls and scenic views around Cabezuela del Valle. Local mountain bikers also love the Cabezuela del Valle a El Torno – vuelta por el Río Jerte, a 29.1 miles (46.8 km) trail leading through the Jerte River valley, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Jerte is defined by river valleys, mountain passes, and oak forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Jerte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 80 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Jerte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Jerte?
A: Jerte offers a wide variety of mountain bike trails, with over 50 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to different skill levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Jerte?
A: The mountain bike trails in Jerte range from easy to difficult. You'll find around 6 easy routes, 28 moderate routes, and 19 challenging routes, providing options for all experience levels.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Jerte?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Jerte are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017881090/los-pilones-to-jerte-circular-through-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos-nature-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones to Jerte - circular through the Garganta de los Infiernos Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018295719/los-pilones-a-jerte-circular-por-collado-de-las-losas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones a Jerte – circular por Collado de las Losas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along mountain bike trails in Jerte?
A: The Jerte Valley is rich in natural beauty. Many trails pass by stunning features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; within the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, and various waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/874276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Trabuquete&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018263167/the-jerte-waterfalls-circular-from-cabezuela-del-valle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Jerte Waterfalls - circular from Cabezuela del Valle&lt;/a&gt; route specifically highlights multiple waterfalls.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the Jerte Valley is dotted with charming villages like Cabezuela del Valle, which is a Historic-Artistic Site. You might also encounter historical structures such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4267031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos V Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6084589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Yuste&lt;/a&gt;, offering cultural points of interest during your rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Jerte?
A: Spring, particularly late March to early April, is a spectacular time due to the 'Cherry Blossom Festival' when the valley transforms into a 'sea of white.' Spring also brings melting snow, feeding the numerous waterfalls. However, the diverse terrain and varied landscapes make Jerte suitable for mountain biking throughout much of the year, with autumn offering beautiful foliage.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Jerte?
A: Yes, Jerte offers approximately 6 easy mountain bike routes that are well-suited for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These trails typically feature less elevation gain and smoother terrain.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in Jerte?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Jerte has about 19 difficult routes. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009926881/cabezuela-del-valle-a-el-torno-vuelta-por-el-rio-jerte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezuela del Valle a El Torno – vuelta por el Río Jerte&lt;/a&gt;, a 46.8 km trail with significant elevation changes through the Jerte River valley.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that feature waterfalls in Jerte?
A: Absolutely. The Jerte Valley is known for its many waterfalls and natural pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018263167/the-jerte-waterfalls-circular-from-cabezuela-del-valle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Jerte Waterfalls - circular from Cabezuela del Valle&lt;/a&gt; route is specifically designed to showcase these natural wonders. You'll also find waterfalls like Chorrero de la Virgen and Caozo Waterfall along various trails.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with scenic viewpoints in Jerte?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Jerte provides numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. Many trails ascend to viewpoints such as Mirador del Valle del Jerte, Mirador de la Serrá, and Mirador del Puerto de Tornavacas, offering panoramic vistas of the valley and surrounding mountains.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Jerte?
A: The mountain bike trails in Jerte are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have explored the area, often praising the diverse terrain, stunning natural landscapes, and well-maintained routes.

Q: Is parking available near the mountain bike trails in Jerte?
A: The Jerte Valley is well-equipped for cyclists, including designated infrastructure. The MTB Center Valle del Jerte, for instance, provides services and information, and you can generally find parking facilities in the villages and at trailheads throughout the valley.
tips: Los Pilones is a beautiful section of La Garganta de los Infiernos where the erosion of the rocks has created wonderful natural swimming pools with small waterfalls. It's a short, sometimes steep walk or MTB ride from the parking lot near the campsites.
At this point the trail connects with the Collado de las Losas trail and several other trails. All wonderful trails to walk or run.
If you're not too tired and it's not too hot, you can probably hike this technical section of the trail. If you don't do it the first time, try again!
